headerEntries: Thavian is a 14th level Charisma caster with a spell save DC of 20 and a +12 bonus

monster.name: Shoth Avatar of Juiblex

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Shoth casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Foul
entries: Any creature, other than an Ooze, that starts its turn within 10 feet of Shoth must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the start of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If Shoth fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Shoth regains 10 hit points at the start of its turn. lf it takes fire or radiant damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. Shoth dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: Shoth can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Shoth makes two Acid Lash attacks.

action.name: Acid Lash
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit +9} to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 60/120 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 5}) acid damage. Any creature killed by this attack is drawn into Shoth's body, here the corpse is dissolved after 1 minute.

action.name: Eject Slime {@recharge 5}
entries: Shoth spews out a corrosive slime, targeting one creature that it can see within 60 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take 44 ({@damage 8d10}) acid damage. Unless the target avoids taking all of this damage, any metal armor worn by the target takes a permanent −1 penalty to the AC it offers, and any metal weapon the target is carrying or wearing takes a permanent −1 penalty to damage rolls. The penalty worsens each time a target is subjected to this effect. If the penalty on an object drops to −5, the object is destroyed. The penalty on an object can be removed by the {@spell mending} spell.

legendary.name: Expand (Turn 1/2)
entries: Shoth can decrease its height to spread itself out up to 50 feet in length and 10 feet in width. When expanded its speed is reduced to 10 feet.

legendary.name: Acid Lash (Turn 3/4)
entries: Shoth makes on Acid Lash attack.

legendary.name: Gaseous Form (Turn 5/6)
entries: Shoth makes can cast {@spell gaseous form}.

legendaryHeader: Shoth can make 3 different boss actions, choosing from the options below. Each action can only be used once per round, and only during the indicated turns of combat. Shoth regains its boss action at the start of the initiative order.

reaction.name: Retaliation
entries: When someone deals radiant or fire damage within 10 feet of Shoth, they must make a {@dc 16} Strength check or be {@condition restrained} by Shoth until the start of its next turn. If they succeed, nothing happens.

reaction.name: Consumption
entries: Shoth can consume any non-hostile Ooze uncontested, when doing so it heals itself equal to the number of hit points the consumed ooze had remaining.

mythic.name: Corrosive Explosion
entries: When Shoth falls to 0 hit points, its body will explode in a 60 foot radius sphere. Everyone within must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 8d10} acid damage, half as much on a save.

entries.name: Shoth's Lair
entries: The aspect has made the Shrine to Shedeklah its home. A strange rift that opened during the demon incursion connecting the Underdark to Shedeklah.

entries.name: Lair Actions
entries: On Initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), The aspect can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; it can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items.name: Acid Cloud
entries: A cloud of acid fills the air within a 10 foot radius from a single point. All who are in the area when it appears or upon entry during their turn must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 4d4 acid damage. Failing the save by 5 or more will cause the creature to take a point of exhaustion.

items.name: Tar Pit
entries: Sticky, tar-like secretions bubble up from the floor covering the lair. All non ooze creatures have their speed reduced to 5 feet.

items.name: Healing Ichor
entries: All ooze creatures in the area may reconstitute themselves from the residue in the lair regaining 3d8 hp.

trait.name: Blind
entries: The vril’b cannot see beyond its blindsight radius and is immune to effects that require sight, such as visual illusions.

trait.name: Echolocation
entries: The vril’b can perceive creatures and objects within 160 feet by emitting high-frequency sound waves. This sense is suppressed while the vril’b is {@condition deafened}.

trait.name: Keen Hearing
entries: The vril’b has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing.

trait.name: Sunlight Sickness
entries: While in sunlight, the vril'b has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws.

If the vril’b remains in direct sunlight for 1 hour or longer, its translucent flesh begins to blister and burn. At the end of each of its turns while exposed to direct sunlight after this time, the vril’b takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage, which ignores resistance.
This damage cannot be reduced or prevented by nonmagical means.

trait.name: Chameleon Flesh
entries: The vril’b has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in dim light, darkness, or rocky subterranean terrain. In such conditions, it can take the Hide action as a bonus action.

trait.name: Alien Physiology
entries: The vril’b has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} and against divination magic used to detect, locate, or read its thoughts.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The vril’b makes two Claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) slashing damage, plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) acid damage.

The target must succeed on a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed} until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this paralysis effect from the vril’b for 24 hours.

action.name: Sonar Blast {@recharge 5}
entries: The vril’b emits a concussive sonic shriek in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 12} Constitution saving throw, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) thunder damage and becoming {@condition deafened} for 1 minute on a failure, or half as much damage and not {@condition deafened} on a success.
A {@condition deafened} creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

reaction.name: Echo Flinch
entries: When the vril’b takes damage from a creature it can perceive with echolocation, it can move up to half its climbing speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

variant.name: Winged Vril’b
entries: Some vril’b retain functional membrane wings.
The vril’b gains a flying speed of 30 ft.
It cannot hover and must end its movement clinging to a surface or fall.
Winged vril’b increase the creature’s effective mobility and ambush potential but do not significantly raise its challenge rating.

Deep within the Underdark’s lightless caverns dwell the Vril’b, bat-like humanoids whose existence is widely attributed to ancient illithid experimentation. Created from goblinoid stock fused with giant cave bats, the vril’b were engineered as living scouts—able to navigate vast tunnel networks through sound alone.

Their nearly translucent skin reveals faint outlines of organs and veins beneath the surface, an unsettling sight for most surface dwellers. Oversized ears and specialized vocal cords allow the vril’b to emit complex sonic patterns, forming a language of vibration, echo, and resonance. Spoken speech is rare among them, and when attempted, produces a harsh, grating rasp likened to stone dragged across glass.

Vril’b society is fragmented and nomadic. They favor high cavern walls, stalactite forests, and vertical tunnel networks where few other creatures can follow. Their acidic neurotoxin—secreted through their claws—paralyzes prey just long enough for a killing blow or swift escape, making them feared ambushers rather than prolonged combatants.

Most vril’b are born wingless, possessing only vestigial membranes. Those rare individuals capable of flight are considered both sacred and cursed, often becoming scouts, exiles, or prized captives among Underdark factions. Illithids, duergar, and drow alike have been known to enslave vril’b for their sensory abilities, though such arrangements rarely last—vril’b memories of their creators run deep, and resentment is etched into their very biology.

Though capable of moving on all fours when hunting or climbing, vril’b commonly adopt a hunched, bipedal stance when interacting with other creatures. In this posture they are able to wear simple clothing, wield tools, and communicate more clearly. Among their kind, standing upright is a sign of peaceful intent.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Yzma is a 7th level spellcaster. Her spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save {@dc 15} spell attacks +7 to hit). She has the following spells prepared:

trait.name: Villainous Preparation
entries: Yzma starts combat under the effects of {@spell mage armor} and {@spell mirror image}.

action.name: Wand of Dramatic Doom
entries: Ranged Spell Attack {@hit 7}, range 60 ft {@h}{@damage 4d8} poison damage, Con save {@dc 14} or {@condition poisoned} until end of next turn.

action.name: Experimental Transformation {@recharge 5}
entries: One creature within 30 ft makes a {@dc 14} Con save or is Polymorphed into a Tiny harmless beast (frog, llama, squirrel) until end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Snide Remark
entries: Yzma can impose disadvantage on one spell attack or saving throw.

legendary.name: Drink Potion
entries: Yzma can regain 10 HP.

reaction.name: Potion Savant
entries: When Yzma would take damage, she can shatter a potion, gaining one effect (roll {@dice d4}):
1. Resistance to that damage type until end of next turn
2. Teleports 15 ft avoiding damage unless the spell is an AOE and the teleport would not remove her from its radius
3. Heals 15 HP
4. Attacker makes a {@dc 14} Con save or is {@condition poisoned}

trait.name: Shoulder Advisors
entries: At the start of each of his turns Kronk consults the devil and angel on his shoulder for advice. See the following table for how he reacts.
Roll {@dice 1d6}:
1–2: Kronk hesitates (disadvantage on all attack)
3–4: No effect
5–6: Kronk is inspired (advantage on all attacks this turn)

action.name: Multiattack.
entries: Kronk makes two attacks, any combination of the following actions plus Wrong Lever! if it has recharged.

action.name: Frying Pan
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Empty Bottle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Wrong Lever! {@recharge 5}
entries: Kronk bumps into or accidentally grabs a full potion bottle and lobs it at the party. Roll {@dice 1d4}:
1. The ground becomes slimy in a 20-foot radius imposing difficult terrain. You must make a {@dc 12} dexterity saving throw to exit the slime.
2. A stinking cloud {@spell stinking cloud} fills a 20-foot radius sphere for 1 round of combat.
3. Roll again, the effect rolled hits Yzma.
4. A {@spell fireball} erupts in a 20-foot radius sphere. This spell always does half damage.

reaction.name: Magic Soak
entries: Kronk can reduce the damage delt to him by a spell by {@dice 1d10 + 3},

reaction.name: Protective Himbo
entries: If Yzma is targeted, Kronk can move up to 15 ft and become the target instead.

monster.name: Stalker of the Horned King

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The stalker casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 15}):

trait.name: Labyrinthine Recall
entries: The stalker can perfectly recall any path it has traveled.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The stalker has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The stalker makes two Glaive attacks or two Rock attacks.

action.name: Glaive
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) force damage.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d10 + 7}) bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 19} Strength saving throw or have the {@condition prone} condition. After the stalker throws the rock, roll a {@dice d6}; on a roll of 3 or lower, the stalker has no more rocks to throw.

action.name: Erupting Horns {@recharge 5}
entries: The stalker causes the earth to churn at a point on the ground it can see within 60 feet of itself. Six horn-shaped stones erupt in a 30-foot-radius, 30-foot-high cylinder centered on that point and then crumble to dust.

Each creature in that area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 33 ({@damage 6d10}) piercing damage and is pushed up to 30 feet upward and then falls. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

Demon worship is rare among stone giants; the destructive tendency of demons is the antithesis of the artistry that drives the stone giant's ordning. The Horned King, Baphomet, is an exception. Though he is a merciless hunter, the Prince of Beasts also crafts complex mazes as his hunting grounds. Stone giants can become mesmerized by the demon lord's mazes and enter his service. These giants can continue pursuing art by crafting mazes, while satiating their bloodlust by hunting in them.

Baphomet rewards his most faithful cultists with transformation into demonic stalkers. Such a giant grows an elaborate crown of six horns, and Baphomet gives the stone giant a magic glaive and the ability to call up horns of stone from the earth.

legendaryHeader: The Stalker can make 3 different boss actions, choosing from the options below. Each action can only be used once per round, and only during the indicated turns of combat. The Stalker regains its boss action at the start of the initiative order.

legendary.name: Charge (Turn 1/2)
entries: The Stalker moves up to her speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack}, then makes a Gore attack.

legendary.name: Glaive (Turn 3/4)
entries: The Stalker makes one Glave attack.

legendary.name: Labyrinth Control (Turn 5/6)
entries: The Stalker can cast {@spell wall of stone}.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Juiblex casts one of the following spells, requiring no material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 20}):

trait.name: Foul
entries: Any creature other than an Ooze that starts its turn within 10 feet of Juiblex must succeed on a {@dc 21} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the start of the creature's next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Juiblex fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Juiblex has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Juiblex regains 20 hit points at the start of its turn. If it takes fire or radiant damage, this trait doesn't function at the start of its next turn. Juiblex dies only if it starts its turn with 0 hit points and doesn't regenerate.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: Juiblex can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Juiblex makes three Acid Lash attacks.

action.name: Acid Lash
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 60/120 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 4d6 + 7}) acid damage. Any creature killed by this attack is drawn into Juiblex's body, where the corpse is dissolved after 1 minute.

action.name: Eject Slime {@recharge 5}
entries: Juiblex spews out a corrosive slime, targeting one creature that it can see within 60 feet of it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw or take 55 ({@damage 10d10}) acid damage. Unless the target avoids taking all of this damage, any metal armor worn by the target takes a permanent −1 penalty to the AC it offers, and any metal weapon the target is carrying or wearing takes a permanent −1 penalty to damage rolls. The penalty worsens each time a target is subjected to this effect. If the penalty on an object drops to −5, the object is destroyed. The penalty on an object can be removed by the {@spell mending} spell.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: Juiblex makes one Acid Lash attack.

legendary.name: Corrupting Touch (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. {@h}21 ({@damage 4d6 + 7}) poison damage, and the target is slimed. Until the slime is scraped off with an action, the target is {@condition poisoned}, and any creature, other than an Ooze, is {@condition poisoned} while within 10 feet of the target.

fluff.name: Juiblex
entries: Called the Faceless Lord and the Oozing Hunger in ancient grimoires, Juiblex is demon lord of slime and ooze, a noxious creature that doesn't care about the plots and schemes of others of its kind. It exists only to consume, digesting and transforming living matter into more of itself.

A true horror, Juiblex is a mass of bubbling slime, swirling black and green, with glaring red eyes floating and shifting within it. It can rise up like a 20-foot hill, lashing out with dripping pseudopods to drag victims into its bulk. Those consumed by Juiblex are obliterated.

entries.name: Cultists of Juiblex
entries: {@note See the {@cult Cult of Juiblex|MPMM} entry.}

entries.name: Juiblex's Lair
entries: Juiblex's principal lair is known as the Slime Pits, a realm that Juiblex shares with {@creature Zuggtmoy|MPMM} (who also appears in this book). This layer of the Abyss, which is also known as Shedaklah, is a bubbling morass of fetid sludge. The landscape is covered in vast expanses of caustic slimes, and strange organic forms rise from the oceans of ooze at Juiblex's command.
Juiblex's challenge rating is 24 (62,000 XP) when encountered in its lair.

entries.name: Lair Actions
entries: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Juiblex can take one of the following lair actions; it can't take the same lair action two rounds in a row:

items.name: Green Slime
entries: A {@hazard green slime} (see the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|DMG}) appears on a spot on the ceiling that Juiblex chooses within the lair. The slime disintegrates after 1 hour.

items.name: Slippery Slime
entries: Juiblex slimes a square area of ground it can see within the lair. The area can be up to 10 feet on a side. When the slime appears, each creature on it must succeed on a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw or fall {@condition prone} and slide 10 feet in a random direction determined by a {@dice d8} roll. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, that creature must make the same save.

The slime lasts for 1 hour or until it is burned away with fire. If the slime is set on fire, it burns away after 1 round. Any creature that starts its turn in the burning slime takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) fire damage.

items.name: Sticky Slime
entries: Juiblex slimes a square area of ground it can see within the lair. The area can be up to 10 feet on a side. When the slime appears, each creature in that area must succeed on a {@dc 21} Strength saving throw or become {@condition restrained}. When a creature enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, that creature must make the same save.

A {@condition restrained} creature is stuck as long as it remains in the slimy area or until it breaks free. The {@condition restrained} creature, or another creature that can reach it, can use its action to try to break free and must succeed on a {@dc 21} Strength check. The slime lasts for 1 hour or until it is burned away with fire. If the slime is set on fire, it burns away after 1 round. Any creature that starts its turn in the burning slime takes 22 ({@damage 4d10}) fire damage.

entries.name: Regional Effects
entries: The region containing Juiblex's lair is warped by its magic, creating one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Acidic Water
entries: Small bodies of water, such as ponds or wells, within 1 mile of the lair turn highly acidic, corroding any object that touches them.

items.name: Corrupted Nature
entries: Within 6 miles of the lair, all Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks have disadvantage.

items.name: Slime
entries: Surfaces within 6 miles of the lair are frequently covered by a thin film of slime, which is slick and sticks to anything that touches it.
If Juiblex dies, these effects fade over the course of {@dice 1d10} days.

monster.name: Fungal Corruption

trait.name: Difficult Terrain
entries: The fungal corruption is considered difficult terrain as large ebony tentacles flail around the area.

trait.name: Tentacle
entries: Anyone who enters within withing 20 feet of the corruption or starts their turn must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning damage and be {@condition restrained} by the tentacles until they save.

A {@condition restrained} creature automatically takes {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning damage at the start of their turn. They make take an action to make a {@dc 19} Strength or Dexterity check to free themselves.

{@i Covered with an iridescent sheen and able to pursue prey on land and water, a purple slime's needlelike spikes seem to be constantly in motion, changing its shape and even its hue to get closer to prey.}

entries.name: Aquatic Lurkers
entries: The purple slime lives and hunts in shallow waters such as ponds, tide pools, abandoned animal troughs, and stagnant, flooded areas, where it is nearly invisible. The creature spreads itself out in such a place and waits for prey to approach.

entries.name: Necrotizing Enzymes
entries: When it detects a nearby creature, it forms long, hollow, conical spikes from its amorphous body which jut out toward the intended prey. Once these spikes pierce its target, it injects a necrotic fluid which dissolves muscle tissue.

entries.name: Patient Hunters
entries: Once a creature is killed or disabled, either by its spikes or its dissolving fluid, the slime undulates forward and covers the creature, devouring it entirely before returning to its previous hiding place.

entries.name: Ooze Nature
entries: A purple slime doesn't require sleep.

action.name: Zuggtmoy Spores
entries: Anytime damage is delt to the corruption Zuggtmoy turns her attention from her combat with Juiblex and forces a cloud of spores to cover an area around the corruption. Anyone within 20 feet of the corruption when this happens must make a {@dc 19} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} poison damage and be {@condition poisoned} until the end of their next turn.

mythicHeader: When the corruption falls to 50% health, Zuggtmoy will force her will deeper into Araumycos causing him to loose the ability to sense who is friend and foe.

mythic.name: Araumycos Defence
entries: At the top of every round until the corruption has been banished Araumycos will summon {@dice 1d4} {@creature void fungus|theabyss} a max of 4 may be summoned at any given time.

monster.name: Dancing Lady
languages: understands Druidic and Sylvan but can't speak

trait.name: Dancing Flowers
entries: The dancing lady has three spinning fungal pods resembling life-sized dancing Humanoids. Each pod has AC 16, 5 hit points, vulnerability to fire damage, and immunity to psychic damage. Attack rolls against these whirling pods have disadvantage. Destroying a pod deals no damage to the dancing lady, who regrows destroyed pods at the end of their turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dancing lady makes two Slam attacks.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Blood-Red Waltz
entries: For each pod the dancing lady has, they can choose one creature they can see within 60 feet of them. Each target must make a {@dc 17} Strength saving throw as a dancing pod sweeps toward them. On a failed save, a creature takes 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage and is moved to an unoccupied space within 60 feet of the dancing lady. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't moved.

action.name: Disarming Quickstep
entries: For each pod the dancing lady has, they can choose one creature they can see within 60 feet of them. Each target must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw as a dancing pod whirls them into a dance. On a failed save, a creature takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) bludgeoning damage and is {@condition dazed|FleeMortals} until the start of the dancing lady's next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition dazed|FleeMortals}.

action.name: Ballet of Terror (1/Day)
entries: The dancing lady's pods pirouette and emit acrid mist. Enemies within 120 feet of the dancing lady who can smell the mist must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes {@condition frightened} of the dancing lady for 1 minute (save ends at end of turn).

reaction.name: Somber Dirge
entries: When a creature the dancing lady can see hits the dancing lady with an attack, the dancing lady causes the attack to hit one of their pods instead.

monster.name: Void Fungus
languages: understands Common and Void Speech but can't speak

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the Void fungus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from a patch of glowing fungus.

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The Void fungus sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Void fungus makes two Poisonous Mycelium or Psychic Blast attacks. If the Void fungus hits one creature with both Psychic Blast attacks, the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw or be {@condition charmed} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

action.name: Poisonous Mycelium
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Psychic Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 5} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) psychic damage.

action.name: Consume Energy
entries: The hair-like tendrils dangling from the Void fungus's cap briefly flash as it draws psychic energy from a creature it can see within 30 feet of it. The target must make a {@dc 13} Charisma saving throw, taking 18 ({@damage 4d8}) psychic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. The fungus regains hp equal to the damage dealt.
A purple glow emanates from the translucent caps of a lavender fungus with pale strands dangling from it. The largest batch of the fungus moves, its dark mycelium reaching up beckoningly.

Void fungus is a semi-sentient cluster of phosphorescent mushrooms that psychically assault living creatures. Once their prey is helpless, they feast on their vital fluids through a fibrous mycelium network.

entries.name: Mobile Fungus
entries: Once a cluster of void fungus has been fed ample blood, one or more swollen mushrooms undergo a metamorphosis, breaking apart from the rest as a creature with enhanced power and mobility. These ambulatory Void fungi collect prey for the colony.

entries.name: Alien Origins
entries: Originating from some long-forgotten corner of the Void, the Void fungus resembles a sinister, otherworldly version of the pixie's parasol mushroom. How their spores spread across the planes is a subject of esoteric debate. This eldritch fungus has been known to turn up in the nightmares of spellcasters, usually alongside even greater threats.

trait.name: Psionic Shroud
entries: Araumycos is immune to divination spells and to any effect that would sense their emotions or read their thoughts.

trait.name: Regeneration
entries: Araumycos's physical body regrows any damage in {@dice 1d4} days.

trait.name: Immortal
entries: Araumycos can only be killed when in a weakened state. To weaken one must first destroy its mind in the astral plane, then its body before. If you destroy its body first, it is impossible to find its astral form.

trait.name: Grey Rapport Spores
entries: To locate Araymycos's astral form, a creature must be inflected by its Grey Rapport spores to enter "The Grey Dream". An astral state that will take you to its astral form.

trait.name: Superior Intellect
entries: Araumycos may concentrate on 2 spells at one time.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Araumycos fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dreamer makes two attacks psionic blast attacks. It may replace one blast attack with a use of spell casting.

action.name: Psionic Blast
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}22 ({@damage 5d8}) psychic damage.

action.name: Psionic Wind {@recharge 5}
entries: Araumycos causes a psionic wind to lash around it in a 120 foot radius sphere. Everyone within 120 feet must make a {@dc 20} Intelligence saving throw or roll {@dice 1d4} for one of the following effects:
1. They become {@condition stunned} for 1 minute - this save may be repeated at the end of each of your turns.
2. {@damage 2d10} psychic damage
3. {@damage 4d10} psychic damage
4. They become {@condition unconscious} until another creature takes an action to shake them awake.

reaction.name: Animate Corpse
entries: When hit with an attack or when a creature dies within 1 mile of Araumycos it may animate a corpse into a {@creature spore servant|theabyss}. The servants only goal is to kill the creature that attacked Araumycos.

legendary.name: Pscionic Scream
entries: Araumycos may expend 1 legendary action to cast {@spell psychic scream}.

legendary.name: Entangle
entries: Araumycos may expend 1 legendary action to cast {@spell cloudkill}.

legendary.name: Fungal Animation
entries: Araumycos may expend 1 legendary action to summon a {@creature shambling mound}. Only one may be summoned at a time.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Araumycos is a 17th level spellcaster and a master of Psionics. Its spell casting trait is Intelligence, and its spell save DC is 20.

mythic.name: Threatened Defence
entries: Once Araumycos has lost half its health, at the top of every round until the Araumycos is no longer threatened it will summon {@dice 1d4} {@creature void fungus|theabyss}. A max of 4 may be summoned at any given time.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spore servant makes two claw attacks.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) slashing damage.

A spore servant is any Large or smaller creature brought back to life by the animating spores of a myconid sovereign. A creature that was never flesh and blood to begin with (such as a construct, elemental, ooze, plant, or undead) can't be turned into a spore servant.

entries.name: Retained Characteristics
entries: The servant retains its Armor Class, hit points, Hit Dice, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, vulnerabilities, resistances, and immunities.

entries.name: Lost Characteristics
entries: The servant loses its original saving throw and skill bonuses, special senses, and special traits. It loses any action that isn't Multiattack or a melee weapon attack that deals bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. If it has an action or a melee weapon attack that deals some other type of damage, it loses the ability to deal damage of that type, unless the damage comes from a piece of equipment, such as a magic item.

entries.name: Type
entries: The servant's type is plant, and it loses any tags.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The servant is unaligned.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Reduce all the servant's speeds by 10 feet, to a minimum of 5 feet.

entries.name: Ability Scores
entries: The servant's ability scores change as follows: Int 2 (-4), Wis 6 (-2), Cha 1 (-5).

entries.name: Senses
entries: The servant has {@sense blindsight} with a radius of 30 feet, and it is blind beyond this radius.

entries.name: Condition Immunities
entries: The servant can't be {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or {@condition paralyzed}.

entries.name: Languages
entries: The servant loses all known languages, but it responds to orders given to it by myconids using rapport spores. The servant gives highest priority to orders received from the most powerful myconid.

entries.name: Attacks
entries: If the servant has no other means of dealing damage, it can use its fists or limbs to make unarmed strikes. On a hit, an unarmed strike deals bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 1d4} + the servant's Strength modifier, or, if the servant is Large, {@dice 2d4} + its Strength modifier.

monster.name: Ront Iceshield
languages: (Abyssal and Giant while attuned to the Glaive of the Horned King)

immune.special: fall damage 100 ft. or less when wearing Impact Plate Armor

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Ront has been blessed by Graz'zt, his spell casting ability is Charisma (spell save {@dc 15}), and he can cast the following spell:

bonus.name: Aggressive
entries: Ront can move up to his speed toward a hostile creature that he can see.

bonus.name: Second Wind (1/Rest)
entries: Ront can regain hit points equal to {@dice 1d10 + 13}.

bonus.name: "I'm The Leader!" (2/Long Rest)
entries: Up to three allies within 120 feet of Ront that can hear him can use their reaction to make one weapon attack and add his Charisma modifier to the attack roll ({@damage +2}).

trait.name: Warrior Trait: Attacker
entries: Ront gets a +2 bonus to all attack rolls.

trait.name: Warrior Trait: Improved Critical
entries: Ront scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

trait.name: Warrior Trait: Battle Readiness
entries: Ront has advantage on initiative rolls.

trait.name: Warrior Trait: Improved Defense
entries: Ront's AC increases by 1.

trait.name: Orc Trait: Indomitable
entries: Once per long rest Ront can reroll a saving throw that fails, but must use the new roll.

trait.name: Graz'zt Boon: Diehard
entries: Ront has advantage on saving throws against effects that cause him to suffer a level of {@condition exhaustion}, and advantage on death saving throws.

trait.name: Orc Trait: Relentless Endurance
entries: Once per long rest, when Ront is reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, he can drop to 1 hit point instead.

trait.name: Inventory: Orcish "Dwarven" Thrower
entries: Ront wields a {@item Dwarven Thrower}

trait.name: Warrior Trait: Rage
entries: In battle Ront can Rage as a bonus action 4 times per long rest. His rage lasts for 1 minute and ends early if he calls {@condition unconscious} or if his turn ends and he hasn't attacked a hostile creature or taken damage.
While raging, he has resistance to all damage except psychic damage

trait.name: Inventory: Impact Plate
entries: Ront is wearing {@item Impact Plate|griffonssaddlebag2}

trait.name: Inventory: Gloom Shield
entries: Ront wields a {@item Gloom Shield|GriffonsSaddlebag3}

trait.name: Inventory: Glaive of the Horned King
entries: Ront wields a {@item Glaive of the Horned King|theabyss}

action.name: Extra Attack
entries: Ront can attack twice, instead of once, whenever he takes the Attack action on his turn.

action.name: Orcish "Dwarven" Thrower Melee
entries: {@hit 15} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@damage 1d8 + 8}) bludgeoning damage in melee, or ({@damage 1d10 + 8}) bludgeoning damage if used with both hands.

action.name: Orcish "Dwarven" Thrower Ranged
entries: {@hit 15} to hit, range 20/60 ft., one target. {@h}({@damage 1d8 + 8}) bludgeoning damage at range plus ({@damage 1d8}) bludgeoning damage or ({@damage 2d8}) if target is a giant. Immediately after the attack, the weapon flies back to Ronts hand.

action.name: Glaive of the Horned King
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 8}) slashing damage, plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) force damage.

action.name: Gloom Shield (3 Charges, Dusk Recharge)
entries: This tall shield is made from the sculpted lid of a coffin. A skeletal figure rests embossed on its surface, reminding would-be attackers of their own mortality.

The shield has 3 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dusk. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges to cast the {@spell Fear} spell from the shield (save {@dc 15}). A creature that fails the saving throw also takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage. Alternatively, when a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with an attack while you're holding the shield, you can use your reaction to expend 1 of the shield's charges to force that creature to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it immediately takes {@damage 4d6} psychic damage and is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn.
Ront was an orc barbarian of the Iceshield tribe, who once roamed the Dessarin Valley. When turmoil swept through the region, the tribe migrated north and made their home near Icespire Peak.
Years later, while out on a hunting expedition, Ront’s entire party was slaughtered by the white dragon Cryovain. Badly wounded and left for dead, Ront was the sole survivor.
In his weakened state, he fell into the Underdark, where he was captured by drow slavers and taken to the prison outpost of Velkynvelve.

monster.name: Spinky

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The spider can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing.

trait.name: Web Sense
entries: While in contact with a web, the spider knows the exact location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

trait.name: Sure-Footed
entries: The spider has advantage on Strength and Dexterity saving throws made against effects that would knock it {@condition prone}.

trait.name: Wooly Cover
entries: The spider's thick wool provides half cover to Medium creatures and three-quarters cover to Small and Tiny creatures that ride on its back.

trait.name: Flammable Fluff
entries: If the spider takes fire damage, it has disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the end of its next turn.

languages: understands common and elvish but does not speak.

action.name: Pick Up Allies
entries: The spider picks up three Medium or smaller willing creatures and places them safely on its wooly back. Creatures considered Tiny do not take up a spot.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) piercing damage, plus and the target must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw, taking ({@damage 6d8}) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. If the poison damage reduces the target to 0 hit points, the target is stable but  {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour, even after regaining hit points, and is {@condition paralyzed} while {@condition poisoned} in this way.

action.name: Web {@recharge 5}
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit +9} to hit, range 60/120 ft., one Large or smaller creature. {@h}The target is {@condition restrained} by webbing. As an action, the {@condition restrained} target can make a {@dc 18} Strength check, bursting the webbing on a success. The webbing can also be attacked and destroyed (AC 10; hp 20; vulnerability to fire damage; immunity to bludgeoning, poison, and psychic damage).

reaction.name: Floof Up
entries: When a creature riding the spider is hit with an attack, the spider can use its reaction to floof up its wool, causing the attacks damage to be reduced by {@dice 1d4}. If the creature is hit with a critical hit, the critical is nullified and {@condition turned|ConfC} into a normal attack. The damage reduction does not apply to critical hits.

With care and nurture, the giant spider egg grows into an enormous wooly spider. This enormous wooly spider is friendly to you and your companions. It will happily carry friendly creatures, as well as equipment up to its carrying capacity, holding everything in its thick wool. Unless trained to fight, it tries to pick up you and your companions on its huge back and run away from any perceived danger as soon as combat begins. It typically does not attack unless trained to, but it will defend itself from hostile creatures.

item.name: Glaive of the Horned King
entries: This massive glaive is forged from blackened abyssal steel veined with dull crimson runes that slowly shift like a living maze. The blade’s edge curves subtly like a horn, and dried blood never seems to fully leave its surface. When held, the weapon feels unnaturally heavy. In addition, you gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Plus it deals an extra {@damage 2d10} force damage to any target it hits.

entries.name: Boons
entries: The glaive grants its wielder {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet. As well as the ability to speak, read, and understand {@language Abyssal}, {@language Giant} and the ability to communicate with {@creature Minotaur}s.
While attuned, its wilder gain advantage on Wisdom {@skill Survival} checks made to track creatures, and they cannot become lost except by magical means unless you are within a labyrinth.

spell.name: Moon Shield
spell.school: Divine boon (1/long rest, no spell slot required)

time.condition: which you take when you or a creature you can see within sight is struck by a critical hit

entries: Silver moonlight flares around the target as Eilistraee's protection answers your call. The triggering attack deals no damage, as though it had missed instead of scoring a critical hit.
Once you use this boon, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
